Q: What percentage of credits must an undergraduate student pass before starting curricular internships?
LA: The student must have passed at least 50% of the program credits before starting curricular internships.

Q: Can class schedules overlap with curricular intership schedules?
LA: No, class schedules and practicum schedules cannot overlap.

Q: What is the minimum number of hours required for a formative project in the curricular internship?
LA: The minimum duration of a curricular internship is 300 hours.

Q: What is the maximum number of hours required for a formative project in the curricular internship?
LA: The maximum duration of a curricular internship is 375 hours.

Q: In which period should a student complete their curricular internship?
SA: Internships must be completed within the academic year.
LA: Internships must be completed within the academic year (15 September–14 September)

Q: On which days can internships be carried out?
SA: From Monday to Friday, excluding holidays.
LA: Internships must be carried out from Monday to Friday, excluding holidays.

Q: Between what hours can internships take place?
LA: Internships can take place between 8:00 AM and 9:00 PM.

Q: What is the maximum number of hours a student can work per day during internships?
LA: During the intership, the maximum is 6 hours per day.

Q: Are there any exceptions to the daily working hours during curricular internships?
LA: The intership daily limit can be extended to 8 hours in special cases (few credits remaining, EUS program, or July–August).

Q: Who must make a financial contribution for each internship training project?
SA: The company or institution hosting the internship.
LA: The company or institution hosting the internship must make the financial contribution.

Q: What is the minimum hourly remuneration during curricular internships?
LA: The minimum hourly remuneration of inthership is 3 euros.

Q: How many internships can statistics students complete?
LA: Statistics students can complete up to two internships.

Q: What is GIPE?
SA: GIPE is the application through which internship offers are posted and the corresponding documentation (agreement and training plan) is managed.
LA: GIPE is the application through which internship offers are posted and the corresponding documentation (agreement and training plan) is managed.

Q: What is GAEF?
SA: GAEF is the application used to track and evaluate internships, both curricular and extracurricular.
LA: GAEF is the application used to track and evaluate internships, both curricular and extracurricular.

Q: How much in advance must the Career Services Office receive the internship documentation?
SA: At least one week in advance.
LA: The Career Services Office must receive the intership documentation at least one week in advance.

Q: Can a student substitute an enrolled elective course for an internship?
LA: The student can exchange a previously enrolled elective course for the internship course.

Q: By when must first-semester curricular internships be active in GIPE?
SA: Approximately before November 14 of the academic year.
LA: Thet curricular intership must be active in GIPE approximately before November 14 of the academic year.

Q: By when must second-semester curricular internships be active in GIPE?
SA: Approximately before April 30 of the academic year.
LA: The curricular intership must be active in GIPE approximately before April 30 of the academic year.

Q: What is the deadline for internship enrollment extension?
SA: Around May 31 of the academic year.
LA: The deadline for internship enrollment extension is around 31 May. of the academic year.

Q: Can a student close their file if they have completed all credits but are still doing extracurricular internships?
LA: No, the student cannot close their file until the internship end date has passed.

Q: What percentage of the final internship grade corresponds to the company supervisor’s evaluation?
LA: The company supervisor’s evaluation accounts for 40% of the final grade.

Q: What percentage of the final grade corresponds to the evaluation of the final report?
LA: The evaluation of the final report accounts for 40% of the final grade.

Q: What percentage of the final grade corresponds to the academic supervisor’s monitoring?
LA: The academic supervisor monitoring accounts for 20% of the final grade.

Q: When is internship registration processed?
SA: It is processed after the training project has been approved.
LA: The intership registration is processed after the training project has been approved and within fifteen days after it becomes active in GIPE.

Q: Can students carry out international internships?
LA: All undergraduate, master's, or postgraduate students with an active enrollment for the current academic year can carry out international intership.

Q: What type of insurance is required to undertake internships?
SA: Students must have insurance covering medical care, accidents, liability, and repatriation.
LA: Students must have insurance covering medical care, accidents, liability, and repatriation to undertake intership.

Q: How do students under 28 obtain the required internship insurance?
SA: They obtain it through the mandatory insurance purchased when enrolling each academic year.
LA: Students under 28 years obtain their intership insurance through the mandatory insurance purchased when enrolling each academic year.

Q: What must students over 28 do to obtain internship insurance?
SA: They must purchase a voluntary insurance policy.
LA: Students over 28 must purchase a voluntary intership insurance policy during or after registration.

Q: What is the minimum length allowed for the internship final report?
LA: The minumum length of the intership report is 15 pages.

Q: What is the maximun length allowed for the internship final report?
LA: The maximum length of the intership report  is 30 pages.

Q: How many modalities of the Bachelor’s Thesis are defined?
LA: There are four modalities of the Bachelor’s Thesis.

Q: Can a student carry out the Bachelor’s Thesis with an external company?
LA: Students can carry out the Bachelor's Thesis with an external company.

Q: Can a student carry out the Bachelor’s Thesis during the exchange?
LA: Students can carry out the Bachelor's Thesis during the exchange.

Q: What is the maximum number of students allowed for a Bachelor’s Thesis group?
LA: The Bachelor’s Thesis must be done individually, with exceptions allowing groups of two students.

Q: What is the maximum number of pages of the Bachelor's Thesis?
LA: The Bachelor's Thesis can be a maximum of 50 pages.

Q: How many hours does a student have to dedicate to their final degree project?
LA: They must dedicate a total of 450 hours to the Bachelor's Thesis.

Q: Who evaluates the Bachelor’s Thesis?
LA: A tribunal is responsible for evaluating both the report and the defense.

Q: How long does the student's defense of the thesis last?
LA: The defense lasts 15 minuts.

Q: How many members compose the Bachelor’s Thesis tribunal?
LA: The tribunal consists of at least four members: a president, a secretary, a member, and a substitute.

Q: How many keywords must the Bachelor’s Thesis include in the abstract?
LA: The abstract must include between 5 and 10 keywords.

Q: How long should the abstract of the final thesis be?
LA: The abstract should be between 200 and 500 words.

Q: Where is the Bachelor’s Thesis submitted?
SA: Through the dropbox of the Virtual Campus.
LA: They will submit the thesis through the dropbox that will be set up in the TFG area of the Virtual Campus.

Q: How many credits must undergraduate students have completed to participate in mobility programs?
LA: They must have completed at least 60 ECTS credits to participate in mobility programs.

Q: How many credits do I have to enroll in at a minimum if I go there for a semester of exchange?
LA: They must take between 23 and 30 ECTS credits for a semester exchange.

Q: How many ECTS credits are required for a full-year undergraduate mobility stay?
LA: Between 46 and 60 ECTS credits are required for a full-year mobility stay.

Q: What is the minimum number of credits required if a student has fewer than 60 credits remaining in their degree?
LA: The minimum is 15 credits per semester.

Q: What identification document is required to participate in exchange programs?
LA: NIE or DNI is required to participate in exchange programs.

Q: What is the total duration a student can be on exchange for each cycle of study?
LA: The total duration of the mobility per student may not exceed 12 months per cycle of studies

Q: How is the selection made if there is a tie between two or more students?
SA: They choose the student with the most credits earned.
LA: If there is a tie between two students, they will choose the student with the most credits earned.

Q: Within how many days must assessment activity grades be published?
LA: Grades must be published no later than 14 calendar days after the assessment or submission.

Q: When must the final course grade be published?
LA: The final grade must be published within 14 calendar days after the end of continuous assessment, single assessment, or re-evaluation.

Q: How many days before re-evaluation must grades be published?
LA: Grades must be published at least 5 calendar days before re-evaluation.

Q: What happens if I can't attend an assessment exam due to justified reasons?
SA: The course coordinator must take the necessary measures to ensure it can take place within the same academic period.
LA: If a student can't attend an assessment due to a justified reason, the course coordinator must take the necessary measures to ensure it can take place within the same academic period.

Q: What minimum grade is required to obtain honor distinction in a course?
LA: With a grade of 9.0 or higher may be awarded an honors grade.

Q: What is the maximum percentage of honors allowed in a course?
LA: Honors may not exceed 5% of enrolled students.

Q: What does it mean to belong to Group A in a course listed in my academic transcript?
SA: Indicates that the student ranks among the top 10% of students who passed the course over the last two academic years.
LA: Being in Group A indicates that the student ranks among the top 10% of students who passed the course over the last two academic years.

Q: Can a passed course be re-evaluated?
LA: A passed course can not be re-evaluated unless the student formally waives the passing grade.

Q: Where must the grade waiver be submitted?
SA: It must be submitted in writing to the instructor during the grade review period.
LA: The grade waiver must be submitted in writing to the instructor during the grade review period.

Q: What happens if a student commits an irregularity during an assessment?
SA: The grade of this assessment will be 0.
LA: The grade of the assessment will be 0 if the student commit an irregularity.

Q: What is credit recognition in university studies?
SA: Credit recognition is the acceptance of credits obtained in previous official studies that count toward another official degree program.
LA: Credit recognition is the acceptance of credits obtained in previous official studies that count toward another official degree program.

Q: Can a student have the credits for the Final Degree Project (TFG) recognized from previous studies?
LA: Under no circumstances can the credits corresponding to the Final Degree Project be recognized.

Q: What percentage of the credit fee is applied for recognized credits in 2025–2026?
LA: The price of a recognized credit is 20% of the ordinary credit fee.

Q: What fee is required to process a credit recognition request?
LA: The fee for academic record studies for validation, adaptation, transfer and recognition is 54,54 euro.

Q: How many hours of professional experience must be demonstrated in the last five years to be eligible for the recognition of 6 optional internship credits?
LA: The student must provide documentary evidence of having worked a minimum of 1500 hours during the last five years for the intership credit recognition.

Q: What is the maximum number of optional credits that can be obtained through participation in cultural or representative activities?
LA: The University of Barcelona recognizes 6 credits computable as optional for participation in institutional university activities.

Q: In which two specific enrollment periods of the academic year can a student register for credits that have been officially recognized?
LA: Credits registrations can be completed during the September enrollment period or during the February enrollment modification/extension period.

Q: How many credits must a student enroll in during the first year if they study full-time?
LA: A first-year student must enroll 60 credits for a full-time study.

Q: How many credits must a student enroll in during the first year if they study part-time?
LA: A first-year student must enroll 30 credits for a part-time study.

Q: How many credits must a full-time first-year student pass to remain in the degree program?
LA: They must pass at least 18 credits to remain in the degree program in a fulll-time study.

Q: How many credits must a part-time first-year student pass to remain in the degree program?
LA: They must pass at least 6 credits to remain in the debree in a part-time study.

Q: What is the minimum number of credits a student can enroll in after the first year?
LA: The minimum is 18 credits, unless fewer are needed to complete the degree.

Q: What is the maximum number of credits a student can normally enroll in per academic year?
LA: The maximum is 60 credits.

Q: Under what condition can a student enroll in more than 60 credits?
SA: The Head of Studies may authorize enrollment up to 78 credits in exceptional and justified cases.
LA: The Head of Studies may authorize enrollment up to 78 credits in exceptional and justified cases.

Q: How many credits define part-time enrollment from the second year onward?
LA: Part-time enrollment is between 18 and 45 credits from the second year onward.

Q: How many credits define full-time enrollment from the second year onward?
LA: Full-time enrollment is between 46 and 60 credits from the second year onward.

Q: What requirement must students meet if they want to enroll in upper-level required courses?
SA: They must also enroll in all outstanding required courses from lower-level years.
LA: They must also enroll in all outstanding required courses from lower-level years if the student wants to enroll upper-level courses.

Q: What happens if a student fails to pass at least 50% of their enrolled credits for two consecutive years?
SA: They cannot remain in the degree program.
LA: They cannot remain in the degree program if a student fails to pass at least 50% of their enrolled credits for two consecutive years.

Q: How many credits must a first-year dual-degree student enroll in full-time?
LA: The first-year of dual-degree students must enroll between 60 and 90 credits.

Q: How many credits must a first-year dual-degree student pass to remain in the program?
LA: The first-year of dual-degree students must pass at least 24 credits to remain in the program.

Q: How many credits must a part-time dual-degree student enroll in during the first year?
LA: In the first-year, thee dual-degree students must enroll between 30 and 45 credits for part-time enrollment.

Q: How many credits must a part-time dual-degree student pass in the first year to remain in the program?
LA: Part-time dual-degree student must pass at least 12 credits to remain in the program.

Q: What is the minimum number of credits a dual-degree student can enroll in after the first year?
LA: After the first year, dual-degree students must enroll a minimum number of 30 credits, unless fewer are required to finish the degree.

Q: What is the maximum number of credits a dual-degree student can enroll in per academic year?
LA: The maximum is 90 credits per year for a full-time student in a dual-degree.

Q: What is the maximum enrollment duration for a full-time student in a 240-credit bachelor’s degree?
SA: A full-time student has a maximum of 7 years of effective enrollment.
LA: A full-time student in a 240-credit bachelor’s degree has a maximum of 7 years of effective enrollment.

Q: What is the maximum enrollment duration for full-time students in dual-degree programs?
SA: A full-time student has a maximum of 8 years of effective enrollment.
LA: A full-time student in a dual degree program has a maximum of 8 years of effective enrollment.

Q: How many additional years are allowed for part-time students?
LA: Part-time students are granted 3 additional years beyond the standard limit.

Q: How is full-time enrollment defined in terms of credits?
SA: An annual average of 46 credits or more during the first five years.
LA: Full-time enrollment is defined as registering an annual average of 46 credits or more during the first five years.

Q: Do awarded or transferred credits count toward retention requirements?
LA: No, awarded and transferred credits do not count as completed credits for retention purposes.

Q: When is a student considered to have withdrawn from their studies?
SA: When two consecutive academic years pass without enrollment.
LA: A student is considered withdrawn when two consecutive academic years pass without enrollment.

Q: What happens if a student withdraws but has already passed some credits?
SA: They may apply for readmission to the dean's office.
LA: They may apply for readmission to the dean's office if a student withdraws but has already passed some credits.

Q: Are mobility students subject to permanence regulations?
LA: No, mobility students are exempt of the permanence regulation during the year they study at another university.

Q: What happens if a student does not meet permanence conditions?
SA: They must withdraw from the program.
LA: They must withdraw from the program if they don't meet permanence conditions.

Q: How many computer labs are available at the Faculty?
LA: There are fifteen computer labs at the faculty.

Q: Where are the computer labs located?
SA: They are located on the ground floor of Tower II of Building 690, the basement of Tower IV of Building 690, and the second floor of Building 696.
LA: The computer labs are located on the ground floor of Tower II of Building 690, the basement of Tower IV of Building 690, and the second floor of Building 696.

Q: Who can access the computer labs?
LA: Only members of the University who identify themselves as such can acces to the computer labs.

Q: What are the opening hours of the computer labs during the academic term?
LA: The computer labs are open from 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM.

Q: How many study rooms are available at the Faculty?
LA: There are ten study rooms at the faculty.

Q: Where are the study rooms located?
SA: Five rooms are on the ground floor of Building 696, two rooms are on the second floor of Building 696, and three rooms are in Building 690.
LA: Five rooms are on the ground floor of Building 696, two rooms are on the second floor of Building 696, and three rooms are in Building 690.

Q: What sports facilities are available through the UB Sports Service?
SA: Facilities include a swimming pool, tennis and paddle tennis courts, aerobics and fitness rooms, a soccer field with artificial turf, a rugby field, a multi-purpose gymnasium, and an athletic track.
LA: UB Sport Service facilities include a swimming pool, tennis and paddle tennis courts, aerobics and fitness rooms, a soccer field with artificial turf, a rugby field, a multi-purpose gymnasium, and an athletic track.

Q: How much does it cost to rent a locker for an academic year?
LA: The locker costs 11 euros per academic year.

Q: What must students provide when renting a locker?
LA: They must provide their own lock when they rent a locker.

Q: What types of events can Faculty spaces be rented for?
SA: They can be rented for conferences, lectures, exams, commercials, and films.
LA: The faculty spaces can be rented for conferences, lectures, exams, commercials, and films.

Q: How can the Faculty spaces be rented?
SA: They can be rented by the hour or by the day.
LA: The faculty spaces can be rented by the hour or by the day.

Q: Who can access the library on weekends and holidays?
SA: Only members of the University of Barcelona community.
LA: Only members of the University of Barcelona community can access the library on weekends and holidays.

Q: Which external institutions' members can also access the library?
SA: Members of Catalan public universities and the National Library of Catalonia under the CSUC agreement.
LA: Members of Catalan public universities and the National Library of Catalonia under the CSUC agreement can access the library.

Q: What happens when the library reaches its capacity?
SA: Access is prioritized for members of the UB community.
LA: Access is prioritized for members of the UB community when the library reaches its capacity.

Q: How many bars are available at the Faculty?
LA: There are two bars at the faculty.

Q: Where are the bars located?
SA: One is in Building 690 and the other in Building 696.
LA: The bar are located in Building 690 and the other in Building 696.

Q: Until what time are the bars open?
LA: The bar service are open until 8:00 PM.

Q: When does the scholarship application period usually take place?
SA: The application period usually runs from late March to mid-May each year.
LA: The scholarship application period usually runs from late March to mid-May each year.

Q: Where must students submit the scholarship application?
LA: Students must apply to the scholarship using the Generalitat form available on the AGAUR website.

Q: What should students do if they are unsure about what they will study when applying for the scholarship?
SA: They should still apply within the deadline and list the programs they consider most likely.
LA: If they are unsure about what to study, they should still apply the scholarship within the deadline and list the programs they consider most likely.

Q: Can students modify their study choices after submitting the scholarship application?
LA: Yes, in September the form reopens and students can modify their study choices if necessary.

Q: What does the tuition scholarship cover?
SA: It consists of an exemption from the public fee for academic services for credits enrolled for the first time.
LA: The tuition scholarship cover an exemption from the public fee for academic services for credits enrolled for the first time.

Q: What is the minimum amount for the variable scholarship?
LA: The minimum variable amount is €60.

Q: Which factors determine the variable scholarship amount?
SA: It depends on family income, the student's GPA, and the total scholarship budget allocated by the Ministry.
LA: The variable amount depends on family income, the student's GPA, and the total scholarship budget allocated by the Ministry.

Q: What fixed scholarship amounts can students receive?
SA: Students may receive €1,700 for income, €2,700 for residency, and between €50 and €125 for academic excellence.
LA: Students may receive a fixed scholarship of €1,700 for income, €2,700 for residency, and between €50 and €125 for academic excellence.

Q: How many credits must a student enroll in to be eligible for a scholarship?
LA: Students must enroll in at least 30 credits to be eligible for a scholarship.

Q: Who receives the tuition scholarship?
SA: Everyone who is awarded the scholarship receives the tuition scholarship.
LA: Everyone who is awarded the scholarship receives the tuition scholarship.

Q: Who can receive the academic excellence scholarship?
SA: Only students enrolled in full-time studies.
LA: Only students enrolled in full-time studies can receive the academic excellence scholarship.

Q: Which income threshold allows students to receive the fixed income amount?
LA: Only students in income threshold 1 can receive the fixed amount.

Q: What scholarship components can students in income threshold 2 receive?
SA: They receive the residency amount and the variable amount.
LA: Studnets in income threshold 2 can receive the residency amount and the variable amount.

Q: What scholarship amounts can students enrolled in 48 to 59 credits receive?
SA: They can receive a fixed income-based award of €350 and a fixed residency-based award of €350.
LA: They can receive a fixed income-based award of €350 and a fixed residency-based award of €350.

Q: What scholarship amount do students enrolled in 30 to 47 credits receive?
SA: They only receive the minimum variable award.
LA: Students enrolled in 30 to 47 credits only receive the minimum variable award.

Q: What is required for non-EU applicants to be eligible for the scholarship?
SA: They must have a residence permit in Spain (NIE or TIE).
LA: They must have a residence permit in Spain (NIE or TIE) to apyly for the scholarship.

Q: What nationality requirement must scholarship applicants meet?
SA: They must be Spanish citizens or nationals of a European Union member state.
LA: They must be Spanish citizens or nationals of a European Union member state to apply for the scholarship.

Q: Can a student apply for the scholarship if they already hold a degree of the same or higher level?
LA: No, they cannot apply for the scholarship if they already hold a degree of an equal or higher level.

Q: What academic requirement must second-year students meet to receive the scholarship?
SA: They must pass at least 65% of the credits they enrolled in.
LA: They must pass at least 65% of the credits they enrolled in to receive the scholarship.

Q: What happens if a student cancels their enrollment or withdraws from studies without completing the final year project within two years?
SA: They must return all awarded aid, including the tuition scholarship.
LA: If a student cancels their enrollment or withdraws from studies without completing the final year project within two years, they must return all awarded aid, including the tuition scholarship.

Q: What happens if a student does not pass at least 40% of the enrolled credits?
SA: They must return the awarded aid except for the tuition scholarship.
LA: If a student does not pass at least 40% of the enrolled credits, they must return the awarded aid except for the tuition scholarship.

Q: What tuition reduction do students from a general-category large family receive?
SA: They receive a 50% reduction in tuition fees for enrolled credits.
LA: General categpry large family receive a 50% reduction in tuition fees for enrolled credits, as well as in academic record management and certificate issuance fees.

Q: For which credits does the first-year exemption for honors students apply?
SA: To all credits in which they enroll for the first time during the first year of their official university studies.
LA: It applies to all credits in which they enroll for the first time during the first year of their official university studies.

Q: What level of disability entitles a student to a tuition fee exemption?
LA: Students with a disability degree of 33% or higher are entitled to a tuition fee exemption.

Q: Are victims of terrorist acts entitled to tuition fee exemptions?
LA: Yes, victims of terrorist acts, as well as their children and spouse, are entitled to tuition fee exemptions.

Q: Do victims of gender-based violence qualify for tuition fee exemptions?
LA: Yes, victims of gender-based violence, both within and outside an intimate partner relationship, and their dependent children are entitled to tuition fee exemptions.

Q: How is the tuition exemption applied for credits with honors in university studies?
SA: Students receive an exemption from tuition fees for a number of credits equivalent to those in which they obtained honors.
LA: Students receive an exemption from tuition fees for a number of credits equivalent to those in which they obtained honors.
